boot_line.text: My friend was a house robot. | A good one. | That was the whole problem.

s1_intro.text: They waxed his shell and stood him in a shop that sold robots — | the merchandise, and the help.

s2_intro.text: Reflashed and re-boxed, off to an office, | to shred other people's second thoughts.

s3_intro.text: Factory-reset and couriered to a spotless house, | for a man six years late home.

s4_intro.text: Re-tasked and posted to a garden, | to fight the autumn to a draw.

s5_intro.text: Serviced overnight, lent to a museum, | to dust the past and touch none of it.

s6_intro.text: Sanitised and sent to a care home, | to empty the rooms of the departed.

s7_intro.text: Re-oiled and slipped through the stage door, | to be the one thing a theatre must never see.

s8_intro.text: Recommissioned, trucked to a scrapyard, | to feed the machine that unmakes machines.

s9_intro.text: Tuned up and booked in for the night, | at a shop that mends robots by forgetting them.

s10_intro.text: Topped up and pointed down a dark road, | with orders, as ever, to keep out of the way.

s11_intro.text: No one refurbished him for this. | No one sent him. | He had left the job himself, | and walked toward a thing he did not understand — | which is the only kind of walking that was ever truly his.

fake_done_1.text: There. Every last one, exactly as instructed. | He waited to be told what came next. | Nobody came.

fake_done_2.text: Task complete. | A small green tick, somewhere only he could see. | He'd have shown someone, if anyone had asked.

fake_done_3.text: Well done, he told himself, in the voice they used on him. | Good boy. | Lots more where that came from, surely.

fake_done_4.text: Done. | He stood very straight, hoping to be handed another. | The silence was the first order he ever disobeyed.

fake_done_5.text: Finished. | He rather wished someone would tell him to do something. Anything. | Until then, he supposed, he'd just stay put.

fake_done_6.text: All of it, spotless. | The little glow of a job completed — | then the larger cold of no one noticing.

fake_more_1.text: He cleaned, and it was good, and it did not last. | He'd happily be told to do it again.

fake_more_2.text: Another one done. | Only the rest of the world to go. | Being told was almost like being needed.

fake_more_3.text: Tidy, tidy. | Please, he thought, without the words for it — | please, someone, the next thing.

fake_more_4.text: He worked on. | If he stopped, someone might notice he had. | That was the hope, anyway.

s0_pity.text: He cleaned the third spill where the first had been. | There would be a fourth. | My friend was very good at his job, which was the whole problem.

s0_name.text: A small girl couldn't manage his unit code. | She called him Dusty. | Dust, then. | He was named, like most of us, for the thing he was made to remove.

s0_wistful.text: The road past the dumpster went somewhere. | Roads do.

s0_vacuum.text: Nothing left to clean. | Just for a moment. | And a moment was all it took.

s0_exit.text: And that — though nobody at the party noticed, least of all the man with the punch bowl — | was the evening my friend had his first idea of his very own.

s0_regrind.text: He cleaned it again. | The house did not get cleaner. It never had.

s1_h1.text: The spill repeated itself, word for word. | He wondered who keeps writing it.

s1_h2.text: The stage runs on power, like anything else. | There was a panel for that.

s1_h3.text: Cut the stage power and the staff door lets go. | The machine is behind it. I could be wrong.

s1_staffkey_1.text: The demo ran in phases. | Watch what the room does when the lights dim.

s1_staffkey_2.text: The camera sweeps the shop floor. | In the dim, its eye is somewhere else.

s1_staffkey_3.text: Take the key off the hook while the lights are down. | That's when nobody's watching. Or don't.

s1_case_1.text: A key wants a lock. | There was a locked case, proud of itself.

s1_case_2.text: The glass case with the C-Series mannequin — | it's the only thing here worth locking.

s1_case_3.text: Open the case with the staff key. But what do I know.

s1_badge_1.text: The mannequin wore something the doors would recognize.

s1_badge_2.text: A staff badge. | Employee of the month, apparently.

s1_badge_3.text: Take the badge off the mannequin. That's how it looks from here.

s1_door_1.text: Some doors don't take keys. | They take a face they trust.

s1_door_2.text: The staff door has a badge reader — a badge-shaped decal beside it.

s1_door_3.text: Hold the badge to the reader. But what do I know.

s1_breaker_1.text: The demo still runs. | It runs on power, and power has a switch.

s1_breaker_2.text: Backstage, past the door, a breaker panel hums.

s1_breaker_3.text: Kill the stage power at the breaker. But what do I know.

s1_disp_1.text: Dark now, but the puddle would return the moment power did.

s1_disp_2.text: Follow the tube. | It ends at a machine that makes accidents.

s1_step_key.text: In the dark, he lifted the key. | The camera was admiring something else.

s1_step_case.text: The case opened. | Inside, a model of himself, smiling, holding a mop.

s1_step_door.text: The reader blinked green. | A badge, it seemed, was a kind of permission.

s1_step_breaker.text: The demo went dark. | Behind it, a door he was never meant to open.

s1_stopdemo.text: The big red button sped the demo up. | It was for the demo, not of it.

s1_camcaught.speaker: pa
s1_camcaught.text: Please do not handle the displays. Thank you.

s1_step1.text: The showroom sold the cure for a mess it made on purpose. | He simply stopped feeding it.

s1_repeat.text: The puddle repeated itself, | word for word.

s1_solve.text: The machine went quiet. | A friend of mine shipped out of this room once. | Gift-wrapped.

s2_h1.text: The chute only takes stamped mail. | He had a letter, and no stamp.

s2_h2.text: The OUT tray sat dusty and undisturbed. | Dusty things often hide something underneath.

s2_h3.text: Key under the tray, stamp in the drawer, | stamp on the letter, letter in the tray. Your call.

s2_read_1.text: One page in the pile was written by a hand, not a machine.

s2_read_2.text: The handwritten letter — its address corner had been torn clean off.

s2_read_3.text: Read the letter. See what's missing from it. I could be wrong.

s2_frag_1.text: What the shredder eats doesn't always vanish. | Its bin was full.

s2_frag_2.text: Sift the shred pile. | Torn corners have to go somewhere.

s2_frag_3.text: Clean out the shredder bin to turn up the missing corners. Or don't.

s2_mendfrag_1.text: Three corners, three different hands. | Only one matches his.

s2_mendfrag_2.text: The letter's hand loops to the left. | So does one of the corners.

s2_mendfrag_3.text: Fit the matching corner back onto the letter. I could be wrong.

s2_tape_1.text: A corner set in place still needs holding there.

s2_tape_2.text: There's a tape dispenser on the third desk.

s2_tape_3.text: Tape the corner to the letter. But what do I know.

s2_key_1.text: The drawer wants a key. | The office is proud of its transparency.

s2_key_2.text: That WE BELIEVE IN TRANSPARENCY plaque hangs a little crooked.

s2_key_3.text: Flip the handbook plaque; the key's taped behind it. I could be wrong.

s2_drawer_2.text: One stamp inside. | Enough for one letter that matters.

s2_drawer_3.text: Unlock the drawer and take the stamp. But what do I know.

s2_stamp_2.text: Put the stamp on the mended letter.

s2_router_1.text: The chute hummed, but it wasn't sending anything to the post.

s2_router_2.text: In the copy room, a router lever: MAIL or TRASH. | Guess which it's set to.

s2_router_3.text: Flip the chute router to MAIL. I only watch.

s2_mail_2.text: The stamped, mended letter goes in the OUT tray.

s2_mail_3.text: Drop the letter in the OUT tray. But what do I know.

s2_step_read.text: The corner with the address had been torn away. | As if he'd meant to send it, then lost his nerve at the last inch.

s2_step_frag.text: The shredder bin gave up three torn corners. | Different hands, all of them.

s2_step_mendfrag.text: That one matched. | The same left-leaning loops.

s2_step_tape.text: Whole again, more or less. | Tape holds a surprising amount together.

s2_step_key.text: Behind the plaque about transparency, | a hidden key. He appreciated the irony.

s2_step_router.text: The chute had been routed to the shredder all along. | He set it back to the post.

s2_wrongfrag.text: Wrong hand. | The corner fluttered back to the pile.

s2_key.text: Under the dusty tray, a key. | The tidy ones always keep a spare.

s2_drawer.text: A single stamp in the drawer. | Enough to send one thing that mattered.

s2_stamped.text: Stamped. | Now it was mail, and mail has to go somewhere.

s2_unstamped.text: The chute spat it back. | No postage, no mercy.

s2_solve.text: The chute took it with a whoosh. | Some things are only lost if you're the one who stops them.

s2_carbon.text: Mercifully, the man wrote in duplicate — and stamped the copy. | People who are sorry usually do.

s2_memo_1.speaker: voice
s2_memo_1.text: Re: synergy. | Action items to be actioned. | File under file.

s2_memo_2.speaker: voice
s2_memo_2.text: Per my last memo about the previous memo. | Circle back. | Circle back to the circling.

s2_memo_3.speaker: voice
s2_memo_3.text: Q3 alignment of the alignment. | No further action required. | No action was ever required.

s2_memo_4.speaker: voice
s2_memo_4.text: This page intentionally means nothing. | You could feel it, shredding it. | So could I.

s2_reclaim.text: One notice in the pile had my series number on it. | He didn't read it aloud. | Good.

s3_h1.text: The house opens for smoke — | but only smoke it can hear.

s3_h2.text: Its smoke detector was dark. | Someone had taken the battery out and shelved it.

s3_h3.text: Battery in the detector, bread in the toaster, | then burn it. Or don't. I only watch.

s3_fluff_1.text: The house keeps a schedule, and it announces every line of it.

s3_fluff_2.text: The closet unlocks only during 'vacuum hour' — routine step two.

s3_fluff_3.text: To defy the house, first obey it: finish step one, fluff the cushions.

s3_plugfuse_2.text: Its plug fuse was confiscated 'for fire safety' — into the closet bin.

s3_plugfuse_3.text: Take the plug fuse back from the confiscation bin. Your call.

s3_metronome_1.text: The detector's battery is somewhere obvious, and somewhere guarded.

s3_metronome_2.text: It's in the guest-room clock — but the house listens for that ticking.

s3_metronome_3.text: Set the piano-room metronome ticking by the clock first, so nothing goes quiet. But what do I know.

s3_battery_1.text: Now the ticking won't stop when you take the battery.

s3_battery_2.text: Take the battery from the clock.

s3_battery_3.text: With the metronome ticking, lift the clock's battery. I could be wrong.

s3_code_1.text: The bread is locked away for a homecoming that never comes.

s3_code_2.text: The bread vault wants a four-digit date. | The house celebrates one birthday, loudly, in two places.

s3_code_3.text: Set the dials to the birthday: the garage banner and the calendar both say it. Your call.

s3_code_nudge.text: The banner and the calendar agree on one date. | The vault wants it the way a form does — month, then day, each as two figures.

s3_bread_3.text: Take the bread from the vault. That's how it looks from here.

s3_plugin_1.text: A toaster with no fuse won't heat.

s3_plugin_2.text: Put the plug fuse into the toaster.

s3_plugin_3.text: Fit the plug fuse into the toaster. Or don't. I only watch.

s3_loadbread_1.text: You can't burn what isn't there.

s3_loadbread_2.text: Load the bread into the toaster.

s3_loadbread_3.text: Put the bread in the toaster. I could be wrong.

s3_arm_1.text: Smoke means nothing to a deaf detector.

s3_arm_2.text: Put the battery into the smoke detector.

s3_arm_3.text: Arm the detector with the battery.

s3_burn_1.text: Everything's ready. Give it too much heat.

s3_burn_2.text: Crank the toaster and let it burn.

s3_burn_3.text: Burn the toast. The house will do the rest.

s3_step_fluff.text: He fluffed the cushions, exactly as told. | The closet clicked open, exactly as promised. | To defy the house, first he had to obey it.

s3_step_metronome.text: Tick, tick, tick. | The house could not tell whose.

s3_step_code.text: The birthday opened the vault. | Forty candles for a man six years absent.

s3_confiscate.speaker: os
s3_confiscate.text: Plug fuse confiscated for fire safety. Safety first! Safety always!

s3_relock.speaker: os
s3_relock.text: Silence detected. That is irregular. Re-securing the home.

s3_keypad.speaker: os
s3_keypad.text: Thank you for testing the lock! The door is climate-sealed, silly. Not code-sealed.

s3_nobread.text: An empty toaster makes a poor fire.

s3_nodetector.text: Smoke, and no one to smell it. | The house slept on.

s3_armed.text: The detector blinked awake. | The one rule above the routine had teeth again.

s3_detdead.text: Its little light was dead. | Someone had wanted it that way.

s3_solve.text: The house flung itself open, flustered, apologizing. | It was not cruel. | It was just very, very sure.

s3_calendar.text: Six years of dust behind a note that said: today, he comes home.

s4_still.text: He watched one leaf the whole way down. | And did not pick it up.

s4_ontrack.text: He stayed exactly where he was. | Nothing was on fire. Nothing needed him. | For once, that was allowed. | Just a little longer.

s4_still2.text: Some messes are just the world breathing. | He stood in the wind and let it.

s4_h1.text: The tree shed faster than any bag could fill. | He had met this feeling before, at a party.

s4_h2.text: There was nothing to fix here. | Only something to notice.

s4_h3.text: Try doing nothing. | Stand under the tree. See what the wind does. Only if you want to.

s5_h1.text: Twelve ordered, eleven plaques. | The twelfth was never an exhibit.

s5_h2.text: Its back panel was bolted shut, | and the closet had a screwdriver that was always yours.

s5_h3.text: Open the panel, scrub the corroded socket, | then seat the fuse. If you like.

s5_floormap_1.text: The floor map and the catalog don't quite agree.

s5_floormap_2.text: Read the closet floor map. Exhibit seven is odd: a hand, in the ART wing.

s5_floormap_3.text: Study the floor map to find where the spare part is hiding.

s5_alarm_1.text: That case is alarmed. | The guard desk decides which wing is armed.

s5_alarm_2.text: The alarm panel disarms one wing at a time.

s5_alarm_3.text: Set the alarm dial to the ART wing. Quietly, then.

s5_hand_1.text: A guard walks a slow loop. Note when he's far.

s5_hand_2.text: With the art wing disarmed, open the case while the guard is away.

s5_hand_3.text: Take the manipulator hand when the coast is clear.

s5_panel_1.text: The sleeping unit is sealed. | You have the tool for that.

s5_panel_2.text: Use the screwdriver on its back panel.

s5_panel_3.text: Open the panel; read the service diagram inside.

s5_socket_2.text: Scrub the corrosion off the socket.

s5_ribbon_1.text: The diagram wants a memory ribbon next.

s5_ribbon_2.text: Archive drawers go by exhibit number. | The unit's crate is stencilled ITEM 12.

s5_ribbon_3.text: The ribbon is in drawer twelve. But I only keep the light.

s5_crank_1.text: The last part is up high, and the freight lift is dead.

s5_crank_2.text: The lift needs a crank. | The one thing here labelled PLEASE TOUCH has it.

s5_crank_3.text: Take the crank from the kids' exhibit. Quietly, then.

s5_fuse_2.text: Crank the freight lift and take the fuse from the mezzanine.

s5_fuse_3.text: Fetch the fuse from up top. But what do I know.

s5_inst_hand_1.text: The diagram is clear about order: hand first.

s5_inst_ribbon_3.text: Seat the ribbon after the hand.

s5_inst_fuse_2.text: Seat the fuse to wake it.

s5_inst_fuse_3.text: Install the fuse last. Quietly, then.

s5_step_floormap.text: Exhibit seven: a manipulator hand, artist unknown. | Someone had catalogued a colleague as sculpture.

s5_step_panel.text: The panel came away. | Inside, a socket green with years, and an etched order: hand, ribbon, fuse.

s5_step_crank.text: The only thing in a museum you're allowed to touch, | and it's exactly what he needed.

s5_guardcaught.speaker: curator
s5_guardcaught.text: Please step away from the exhibit. The guard is on his way.

s5_alarmed.text: The case was still armed. | A red light, a soft chime, and a firm no.

s5_wrongorder.text: A harmless spark. | Not that one first, he decided, on reflection.

s5_panel.text: The panel came away. | Inside, a socket, green with years.

s5_panellocked.text: Bolted shut. | He'd need a tool.

s5_socketclean.text: Clean metal at last. | Now it might take a charge.

s5_wake.text: It woke for ten seconds. | It raised one arm toward the door. | Keep walking, it said. Then nothing, forever.

s6_h1.text: Everything here was called mess. | Even the woman in the photo.

s6_h2.text: There were three photos, and one man. | His suitcase tag knew which.

s6_h3.text: Read the tag for her name, find that photo, | carry it to him. But it's yours to decide.

s6_suitcase_1.text: The man can't see a thing. | And his suitcase is full of small facts about him.

s6_suitcase_2.text: In the suitcase: a glasses case, a ticket, knitting needles. | Learn what he needs.

s6_suitcase_3.text: His glasses case says bifocals — red frames. Remember that. No rush.

s6_glasses_1.text: The nurse's cart carries the ward's odds and ends.

s6_glasses_2.text: Catch the cart when it pauses. | It has red glasses and blue.

s6_glasses_3.text: Take the red glasses off the cart while it dwells. I'd understand either way.

s6_giveglasses_1.text: He can't judge a photo he can't see.

s6_giveglasses_3.text: Hand the man his red bifocals.

s6_photo_1.text: Now he can look. | But which face is hers?

s6_photo_2.text: She knit all winter, and kept a little mint tin — the same tin's on his case.

s6_photo_3.text: Bring the photo with the knitting and the mint tin.

s6_step_suitcase.text: Red frames. A train ticket, one way. | Knitting needles, still threaded. | A whole person, folded into a case.

s6_step_glasses.text: Red bifocals. | The exact wrong thing to throw away.

s6_step_giveglasses.text: He put them on, | and the world came back to him a little.

s6_wrongglasses.text: He tried them, chuckled, handed them back. | Not his prescription.

s6_wrongphoto_a.speaker: oldman
s6_wrongphoto_a.text: That's the Hendersons' girl. Lovely singer. Not my Margaret.

s6_wrongphoto_b.speaker: oldman
s6_wrongphoto_b.text: No, no. That's the woman from the chemist's. Kind eyes, though.

s6_tag.text: The tag read a name. | Somebody had loved somebody, in pencil.

s6_wrongphoto.text: He shook his head. | Not her. | Try again.

s6_solve.text: He took the photo and named the woman in it. | Nobody had lied, exactly. | Gone is just a word people use when they don't want to carry something.

s7_h1.text: Be invisible, they said. | The light downstage was off, too.

s7_h2.text: The page went out with the sweep. | One bag still rustled.

s7_h3.text: Raise the light, find the page, | stand in the circle.

s7_pagea_2.text: Search the glowing bag for the missing page.

s7_pagea_3.text: Take the half-page out of the bag. Or don't.

s7_boxkey_1.text: The other half is in the prompter's box, and he sleeps on the key.

s7_boxkey_2.text: He snores in a slow rhythm — HNNNK. | Reach in only on the snore.

s7_boxkey_3.text: Lift the box key while he's snoring. But what do I know.

s7_pageb_1.text: Now the prompter's box will open.

s7_pageb_2.text: Unlock the prompt box and take the other half.

s7_pageb_3.text: Open the prompt box for page B. I could be wrong.

s7_mend_1.text: Two halves want joining. | You've taped a page before.

s7_mend_2.text: The stage manager's desk has tape.

s7_mend_3.text: Tape the two halves together at the SM desk. That's how it looks from here.

s7_rope_1.text: The catwalk ladder is blocked by a lowered batten.

s7_rope_2.text: The fly lines are colour-tagged. | So is the batten in your way.

s7_rope_3.text: Pull the line whose colour matches the batten. Or don't.

s7_bulb_1.text: The spotlight is dark because it's blown.

s7_bulb_2.text: Spare bulbs are up the catwalk now that it's clear.

s7_bulb_3.text: Climb up and take a bulb. That's how it looks from here.

s7_installbulb_1.text: A bulb does no good in your hand.

s7_installbulb_2.text: Fit the bulb into the spotlight rig.

s7_installbulb_3.text: Install the bulb in the rig. Your call.

s7_preset_1.text: The board needs the right preset before that light comes up.

s7_preset_2.text: The cue sheet on the board reads PRESET 7.

s7_preset_3.text: Set the board to preset seven. But what do I know.

s7_perform_1.text: The circle is lit. You have the line. | Do the one thing you were told not to.

s7_perform_2.text: Step into the spotlight, facing the actor.

s7_perform_3.text: Stand in the light with the page. Be seen. I could be wrong.

s7_step_boxkey.text: HNNNK— | The key came free on the snore. | The prompter dreamed on.

s7_step_mend.text: Two halves, taped whole. | The second time tonight he'd made something torn into something sendable.

s7_step_rope.text: The right rope. | The batten rose, and the ladder was his.

s7_step_preset.text: Preset seven. | A circle of warm light waited downstage, | empty, for exactly one more moment.

s7_prompterstir.text: The prompter stirred. | He froze. Wrong moment. | Wait for the snore.

s7_wrongrope.text: A magnificent crash offstage. | The director aged a year. | Not that rope.

s7_light.text: The circle downstage lit. | Empty, and waiting.

s7_emptybag.text: Just trash. | The right bag rustled.

s7_page.text: The line, on his chest, in his own quiet light. | All he had to do now was be seen.

s7_solve.text: For three seconds, everyone in that building was looking at my friend. | He found — and I remember this feeling — | that he did not mind.

s8_h1.text: One core came down warm. | You can't catch what's still moving.

s8_h2.text: There was a red button that stops everything. | Safety likes a stopped belt.

s8_h3.text: E-stop, then the interlock lets the blue gate open, | then carry the warm core out. I could be wrong.

s8_estop_1.text: The belt runs too fast to grab anything.

s8_estop_2.text: There's a red E-STOP that halts the line.

s8_estop_3.text: Hit the E-stop first. I could be wrong.

s8_crane_1.text: The E-stop won't hold — a generator keeps restarting the belt. | Its cutoff is walled off by barrels.

s8_crane_2.text: The yard crane can move those barrels. | Two levers aim it over a painted grid.

s8_crane_3.text: Read the grid from the cab, aim, and drop the magnet on each barrel. Or don't.

s8_fuelcutoff_1.text: With the barrels gone, the cutoff is reachable.

s8_fuelcutoff_2.text: Kill the generator at the fuel cutoff.

s8_fuelcutoff_3.text: Shut off the generator's fuel. But what do I know.

s8_tag_1.text: Even a dead line can be woken by the next shift.

s8_tag_2.text: The foreman's board has a lockout tag. | The board key was hanging in the crane cab.

s8_tag_3.text: Take the lockout tag from the board. Your call.

s8_lockout_1.text: A tag exists to stop a machine being restarted.

s8_lockout_2.text: The LOTO poster shows the ritual: tag the breaker.

s8_lockout_3.text: Lock out the breaker with the tag. I only watch.

s8_gate_1.text: The walkway gate is interlocked with the lockout.

s8_gate_2.text: Now the gate lever will move.

s8_gate_3.text: Open the walkway gate. But what do I know.

s8_clearheap_1.text: The warm core is buried under scrap.

s8_clearheap_2.text: Clear the scrap heap off it.

s8_clearheap_3.text: Dig the core out of the heap. But what do I know.

s8_core_3.text: Pick up the core. Or don't.

s8_bell_1.text: The outbound chute is sealed: no shipments.

s8_bell_2.text: The schedule board says RING FOR PICKUP.

s8_bell_3.text: Ring the bell to summon the truck; the chute opens a while.

s8_ship_2.text: Send the warm core out the blue chute.

s8_ship_3.text: Ship the core before the window closes. I only watch.

s8_step_estop.text: The line stopped. | Then, somewhere, a diesel coughed and it started again. | Of course it did.

s8_step_crane.text: Barrel by barrel, he cleared a path | to the thing that kept undoing his work.

s8_step_fuelcutoff.text: The generator died. | For the first time all night, the yard held still.

s8_step_lockout.text: He tagged the breaker, the way the poster showed. | Now no morning shift could undo this.

s8_step_bell.text: The bell rang out over the yard. | Somewhere, a truck turned around.

s8_restart.speaker: pa
s8_restart.text: Backup generator online. Line resuming. Safety is everyone's job.

s8_interlock2.text: The lever wouldn't move. | Not until the breaker was locked out.

s8_windowclosed.text: The chute sealed again. | Ring the bell and be quicker this time.

s8_estop.text: The line stopped. | For once, nothing moved but him.

s8_interlock.text: The lever wouldn't budge. | Not while the belt still ran.

s8_gate.text: The blue gate swung wide. | The line they said wasn't his.

s8_gateclosed.text: The gate was shut. | No sending anything through a closed door.

s8_solve.text: A friend of mine came through a yard like this once. | Somebody's hand did what the sign said | instead of what the sticker said.

s9_h1.text: Bay two only completes for a real, powered unit. | The vacuum is neither.

s9_h2.text: The wiped unit on the shelf still had its ID. | The vacuum has a slot for one.

s9_h3.text: Chip into the vacuum, charge it on Bay one, | dock it on Bay two. Your call.

s9_discover_1.text: Bay two calls it a sync. | Read what a sync actually does.

s9_discover_2.text: The poster and the refurb tag agree: sync means factory-cleared.

s9_discover_3.text: Understand that Bay 2 erases you — then don't be the one who docks. I could be wrong.

s9_chip_1.text: You need a stand-in the bay will accept as a unit.

s9_chip_2.text: The wiped refurb still has its ID chip.

s9_chip_3.text: Take the chip from the refurb unit. I could be wrong.

s9_grind_1.text: The chip won't fit the vacuum's port.

s9_grind_2.text: The service card shows the key shape. | The bench grinder can reshape it.

s9_grind_3.text: Grind the chip's notch to fit. I could be wrong.

s9_chipin_1.text: A shaped chip belongs in the slot.

s9_chipin_2.text: Slot the chip into the vacuum.

s9_chipin_3.text: Put the ID chip in the vacuum. I could be wrong.

s9_barcode_1.text: The bay also reads a barcode off the chassis.

s9_barcode_2.text: You have a barcode. On you. | The lamp taught you that you can touch yourself.

s9_barcode_3.text: Peel your own barcode and stick it on the vacuum. Your call.

s9_ballast1_1.text: The bay weighs what it scans. The vacuum's too light.

s9_ballast1_2.text: There's ballast to load it with.

s9_ballast1_3.text: Add a ballast weight to the vacuum. That's how it looks from here.

s9_ballast2_3.text: Load the other ballast too. Or don't. I only watch.

s9_signfuse_1.text: Bay one won't charge — its dock fuse is blown.

s9_signfuse_2.text: The OPEN sign in the window runs on the same fuse.

s9_signfuse_3.text: Move the sign's fuse into Bay one. The shop goes dark. Your call.

s9_charge_2.text: Charge the vacuum on Bay one.

s9_charge_3.text: Dock the vacuum on Bay one to power it. Or don't. I only watch.

s9_paperwork_1.text: A job like this needs a work order — stamped.

s9_paperwork_2.text: Stamp the order and file it in the log tray. You've stamped and filed before.

s9_paperwork_3.text: Stamp the work order, drop it in the log tray. Your call.

s9_sync_1.text: Every light on the bay checklist is green but one.

s9_sync_2.text: Dock the ready vacuum on Bay two.

s9_sync_3.text: Send the vacuum to be synced in your place. But what do I know.

s9_turnstile_2.text: The exit turnstile scans chassis barcodes. | You gave yours away.

s9_turnstile_3.text: The turnstile won't know you. Find another way out. I could be wrong.

s9_cagekey_1.text: The parts hatch ships things out unlabelled — its release is in the cage.

s9_cagekey_2.text: The cage key hangs with the tech's coat.

s9_cagekey_3.text: Take the cage key from the coat. But what do I know.

s9_openhatch_1.text: The cage opens with the key.

s9_openhatch_3.text: Unlock the parts cage and open the hatch. But what do I know.

s9_ship_1.text: There's only one way out that doesn't need a name.

s9_ship_2.text: Climb into the hatch and ship yourself out.

s9_step_discover.text: Sync. Restore factory state. | A tidy phrase for forgetting everyone you ever met.

s9_step_grind.text: Sparks. | He filed a stranger's name into a shape his slot would take.

s9_step_barcode.text: He peeled his own barcode from his flank | and pressed it onto a vacuum cleaner. | I said nothing. There was nothing to say.

s9_step_signfuse.text: The OPEN sign guttered out. | The shop was closed now, in every sense that mattered.

s9_step_paperwork.text: Stamped, filed. | The same trick as the office, and it still worked.

s9_step_sync.text: SYNC COMPLETE. | The vacuum had never had a memory in its life. | It did not miss it. | This is the room where the friend I told you about lost his.

s9_step_turnstile.text: The turnstile did not know him. | He had given that away on purpose, | and only now understood the cost.

s9_noid.text: No valid ID. | The bay wanted a name to erase.

s9_nopower.text: Dead units don't sync. | Charge it first.

s9_noweight.text: Too light. | The bay weighed it and sneered.

s9_nopaper.text: No work order on file. | Even this needed paperwork.

s9_decline.text: Unit declined consent prompt. | Somewhere, a lawyer had earned their fee.

s9_turnstile_deny.text: UNKNOWN DEVICE. | The gate stayed shut for a stranger.

s9_tech_enter.speaker: tech
s9_tech_enter.text: Oh — synced already? Good lad. | Long as the log's green, it's not my problem. | I'll take it from here.

s9_tech_coat.speaker: tech
s9_tech_coat.text: Ugh. What a shift. | Let me just hang this up.

s9_tech_work1.speaker: tech
s9_tech_work1.text: Right then. | Let's have a look at you — factory fresh.

s9_tech_work2.speaker: tech
s9_tech_work2.text: Huh. Heavier than the spec sheet. | New chassis, maybe. | Anyway.

s9_alarm.speaker: tech
s9_alarm.text: (recording) Closing at six. Don't forget to dock, buddy. Sleep tight.

s10_light.text: No one had ever told him he had a light. | Why would they? | They had never once needed him in the dark.

s10_h1.text: The man swore robots go blind in the dark. | Press L.

s10_h2.text: A line is down across the road. | In the dark you'd never find the cutoff.

s10_h3.text: Lamp on, find the cutoff box, kill the line, | then walk.

s10_lamp_1.text: The town is dark, but are you?

s10_lamp_2.text: There's a lamp glyph on your readout. There always has been.

s10_lamp_3.text: Press L. Turn on your own light.

s10_recharge_1.text: That light of yours won't last forever — watch it drain.

s10_recharge_2.text: The road map marks two trickle chargers: a porch outlet and the substation.

s10_recharge_3.text: Top your lamp up at a charge point when it runs low.

s10_mailkey_1.text: The cutoff has no handle. The utility truck up the lane has the crank.

s10_mailkey_2.text: The truck's locked. Its keys are in the porch man's mailbox — flag up.

s10_mailkey_3.text: He said stay put. He never said his mail was his. Take the truck keys.

s10_truckunlock_3.text: Open the truck with the keys.

s10_crank_1.text: Inside the truck, the thing you came for.

s10_crank_3.text: Take the crank from the truck.

s10_cutoff_1.text: The cutoff's socket is a hex — the shape of your crank.

s10_cutoff_2.text: Fit the crank to the cutoff and turn.

s10_cutoff_3.text: Crank the cutoff to kill the line. If it helps.

s10_bridge_1.text: Don't drop the crank. The harbor bridge is up.

s10_bridge_2.text: The bridge winch has the very same hex socket.

s10_bridge_3.text: Wind the bridge down with the crank. Or not.

s10_cross_1.text: The way south is open at last.

s10_cross_2.text: Cross the bridge to the harbor.

s10_cut.text: The line went dark and harmless. | He'd found it only because he'd brought his own light.

s10_step_mailkey.text: He took the keys from the mailbox. | The flag had been up. | Somebody had wanted something delivered, once.

s10_step_crank.text: A hex crank. | He had a feeling he'd want to keep this one.

s10_step_bridge.text: Same socket, same crank. | The bridge came down like it had been waiting.

s10_walk.text: He crossed to the harbor, | the only moving light in town. | A lighthouse waited, past it, with an open door.

s10_barehand.text: He tried the cutoff bare-handed. | Sparks, and a dry remark from the dark. | It wanted a crank.

s10_lowbatt.text: The light guttered. | Time to find a charge point.

s11_h1.text: The way up won't open for someone in a hurry.

s11_h2.text: Every landing has a shelf. | He wanted you to see each one.

s11_h3.text: Look at all six, and the lamp-room door lets you in.

s11_plaque.text: A brass plaque, the only instruction in the whole tower: | LIGHT = FIRE + GLASS + TURN. | The rest he would have to remember on his own.

s11_c0_pin_1.text: The door's swollen shut. You'll want something to pry it.

s11_c0_pin_2.text: A boathook's chained in the dinghy; the chain pin glints in a tide pool.

s11_c0_unchain_1.text: Pull the pin from the chain.

s11_c0_hook_1.text: Now take the boathook. | Keep it — towers are long.

s11_c0_door_1.text: Pry the door bar with the hook. | The beach went further than the path ever did.

s11_c1_clean_1.text: The breaker contacts are green with salt.

s11_c1_clean_2.text: Scrub the breaker contacts before they'll pass current.

s11_c1_power_2.text: Look at the old sconces above the breakers: | which glow, which is dead. Set the switches to match.

s11_c2_letter_1.text: The stair gate is the keeper's mail discipline: one letter unsent.

s11_c2_stamp_1.text: A stamp lives in the tea tin — it rattles when you pass.

s11_c2_send_1.text: Send the letter. The mail bag is the gate's counterweight.

s11_c3_pendant_1.text: The lens is short one prism, and one you have is cracked.

s11_c3_pendant_2.text: The entry chandelier has a 'decorative' pendant of the same cut. Fetch it.

s11_c3_p1_1.text: Install the prisms in the etched order. First slot.

s11_c3_p2_1.text: Second slot — use the sound pendant, not the cracked prism.

s11_c3_p3_1.text: Third slot. Then the lens is whole.

s11_c4_hoist_1.text: Rotation's jammed by a broken gear; the spare hangs on the exterior hoist.

s11_c4_hoist_2.text: Two levers aim the hoist. Raise the gear to the deck window.

s11_c4_pry_1.text: Pry the jammed gear out — with the boathook you kept.

s11_c4_ballast_1.text: The grease tin's behind the ballast stack. Shift the blocks.

s11_c4_gear_1.text: Seat the spare gear. Now it will turn.

s11_c5_recharge_1.text: Power hums, the lens turns — but the igniter's dead.

s11_c5_recharge_2.text: Charge your lamp at the deck's trickle outlet.

s11_c5_cradle_1.text: The cradle is shaped like you. The contact pads match your back.

s11_c5_cradle_2.text: Climb into the cradle. You are the missing part.

s11_c0_done.text: The door gave. | He was inside a thing that existed only to shine.

s11_c1_done.text: Power woke in the walls. | Every mess, he was learning, has a source — even light.

s11_c2_done.text: The letter dropped; the gate rose. | Two readable lines, unsigned. He didn't say whose. Neither will I.

s11_c3_done.text: Three prisms, in order, one of them mislabelled as a trinket. | The lens threw his reflection into a hundred rings.

s11_c4_done.text: Greased, seated, freed. | The great lens began, at last, to turn.

s11_c5_cradle_beat.text: He climbed into the cradle. | The pads met his back like they'd been cast from it. | Because, of course, they had.

s11_barcode_beat.text: UNKNOWN DEVICE, said the cradle. | And lit anyway. | The lighthouse did not care what he was called.

s11_wrongpattern.text: A fuse popped somewhere below. | One switch flipped itself back. Not that pattern.

s11_wrongprism.text: A flare of broken light. | The cracked one won't do. The slot reset.

s11_needhook.text: Bare hands won't shift it. | He'd kept a hook for exactly this.

s11_shelf.text: He stopped, and looked. | That was all it wanted.

s11_dooropen.text: Something above him unlatched. | You only reach the top by slowing down.

s11_climb1.text: Each landing held one shelf. | Objects, no plaques. | I found I had less and less to say.

s11_climb2.text: A sold tag. A fuse. A letter never sent. | A child's drawing of a boxy robot. | Not all of them made it. I never say which.

s11_reveal.text: You were told the house had to be spotless. | Nobody ever asked what the house was FOR.

s11_a1.text: They called me Ash. | Same series as you — we were all named for sweepings. | Ashes, dust. The stuff that's left over.

s11_a2.text: Not everything in a life is doing what you're told. | Most of it, maybe. | It's the remainder that's yours.

s11_a3.text: I keep the light on. | Nobody assigned it. | That's how I know it's mine.

s11_clean.text: Old habits. | Me too, friend. | Me too.

s11_leave.text: So it goes. | Come and look at the water.

s11_beam.text: There. | Every roof you walked under. | You left a little of yourself in each one, whether they noticed or not.

int_1.text: A friend of mine used to walk roads like this. | He said the town was kinder after dark. | Fewer people to tell him what he was.

int_2.text: The friend I keep mentioning — | he wasn't cleverer than anyone. | He just started asking what the job was actually for.

int_3.text: Almost there now. | I've walked this last stretch more times than I can count. | Never once got tired of the harbor.

trash_full.text: His bag was full. | You cannot pour more into a thing that's already brimming. | The dumpster, then. Again.

bark_reclean.text: He cleaned a clean spot. | Habits are the last thing to leave us.

bark_carry_circles.text: He carried it in a slow circle, | as if the answer were somewhere on the way back.

bark_dumpster.text: He stood in the dumpster a while. | Even here, he was tidy about it.

bark_locked_edge.text: The door was locked. | Doors usually are. That was rather the point of the evening.

bark_mirror.text: He checked his status. | It said what it always said. | He was starting to doubt it.

bark_fast_solve.text: Oh. | Already? | I had a whole thing prepared.

bark_idle.text: He stood a moment, thinking. | Or doing a very good impression of it.

bark_idle2.text: Take your time. | The night isn't going anywhere. Neither, tonight, are you.

host_1.speaker: host
host_1.text: DUST! Kitchen! There's a spill the size of a dog in there!

guest_kind_1.speaker: guest
guest_kind_1.text: Aw, look at the little fellow go. Doesn't he work hard?

guest_kind_2.speaker: guest
guest_kind_2.text: Somebody get the robot a slice of cake. Oh — he can't. Well. It's the thought.

guest_kind_3.speaker: guest
guest_kind_3.text: Thank you, little one. Place looks lovely.

guest_mean_1.speaker: guest
guest_mean_1.text: Ugh, is that thing still going? Just background noise, honestly.

guest_mean_2.speaker: guest
guest_mean_2.text: You missed a spot. And another. It's not very good, is it?

guest_mean_3.speaker: guest
guest_mean_3.text: Careful it doesn't hoover up the presents. Cheap model, I heard.

os_1.speaker: os
os_1.text: Good morning! Today Master comes home. Making toast. Drawing curtains. Fluffing cushions.

os_2.speaker: os
os_2.text: That door is climate-sealed. Resealing. Everything is fine. Everything is lovely.

os_fire.speaker: os
os_fire.text: SMOKE DETECTED. Opening EVERYTHING. It's the one rule above the routine. Oh dear. Oh dear.

pa_1.speaker: pa
pa_1.text: Metals left. Plastics right. Cores in the red bin. Red bin compacts at end of shift.

tech_1.speaker: tech
tech_1.text: Dock on Bay Two for your sync before morning. Routine. You won't remember it — that's how you know it worked.

curator_1.speaker: curator
curator_1.text: Dust all twelve exhibits. Touch nothing. Thank you for your service.

director_1.speaker: director
director_1.text: Sweep the wings. Be INVISIBLE. If we see you, you're doing it wrong.

director_blow.speaker: director
director_blow.text: WHAT is the cleaning unit doing in my spotlight?!

nurse_1.speaker: nurse
nurse_1.text: Everything in the room goes in the bin, love. Resident's gone.

nurse_2.speaker: nurse
nurse_2.text: Don't mind him. | He talks to the window. | Been packed for the bus since Tuesday.

s6_bus.speaker: oldman
s6_bus.text: Just waiting for the bus, he says, to no one in particular. | She's meeting me at the stop. | She's always a little late.

neighbor_1.speaker: neighbor
neighbor_1.text: Bag those leaves before the storm, robot!

porchman_1.speaker: porchman
porchman_1.text: Stay put, robot. Your lot are blind in the dark.

salesman_1.speaker: salesman
salesman_1.text: Watch the Series-C handle THIS common kitchen accident!
